title: Leaf surface acylsugar extraction and LC-MS profiling - v1.0
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b7imh","text":"We use this protocol to extract and study acylsugars from glandular trichomes present on the surface of Solanum lycopersicum and Solanum pennellii plants as described in our manuscript \"Evolution of metabolic novelty: a trichome-expressed invertase creates specialized metabolic diversity in wild tomato\" (doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/502971).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":104,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":129,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":311,"length":30}],"data":{}},{"key":"aotao","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7f005","text":"While this method is optimized for acylsugar extraction, it can be tweaked to use for other metabolites on the leaf surface.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1gsn3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4emv3","text":"Below is a literature that is highly useful for acyslugar identification:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8v2av","text":"Comparative structural profiling of trichome specialized metabolites in tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) and S. habrochaites: acylsugar profiles revealed by UHPLC/MS and NMR (doi: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11306-013-0585-y)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":80,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":88,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":106,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":177,"length":41}],"data":{}},{"key":"bk41d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8467507023EF11E9A87E8534AA35DEC0","url":"https://doi.org/10.1101/502971"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E030A0F023EF11E9A2E8F32E236663DD","url":"https://doi.org/10.1007/s11306-013-0585-y"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"d5jqo","text":"LC-MS Analysis","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ckv3q","text":"If you are not familiar with LC-MS, we highly recommend getting on-site help from an experienced person. Do not attempt to set up LC-MS analysis merely based on this protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8ooq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1gchu","text":"Glassware","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b931b","text":"Carefully wash the glassware used for LC-MS solvent and acylsugar extraction solution preparation to remove contaminants that might interfere with LC-MS analysis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7vcm","text":"Our standard protocol calls for six times thorough rinses with water to avoid detergents, and several round of rinsing with appropriate organic solvent. While other contamination might occur under different circumstances, we suggest checking with your LC-MS specialists or core facility manager for preferred cleaning protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fill5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"471ql","text":"Extraction Tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8aumf","text":"We use Micrew tube with O-ring seal (1.5 mL) extraction vial for our study. Snap-lid polyethylene microcentrifuge tubes are compatible too.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"27gb0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"452v2","text":"Solvent and Solution Storage","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1epd5","text":"We suggest always making fresh solvent and solution. However, in our experience, extraction buffer could be stored short term at 4℃ or -20℃ with no obvious issues. We did not carry out degradation curve and do not claim to know the best storage condition.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ftdnv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"540ah","text":"Acylsugar Identification","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1ltk","text":"For more information, please see:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"da4ud","text":"Evolution of metabolic novelty: a trichome-expressed invertase creates specialized metabolic diversity in wild tomato\" (doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/502971).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":125,"length":30}],"data":{}},{"key":"5n837","text":"Comparative structural profiling of trichome specialized metabolites in tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) and S. habrochaites: acylsugar profiles revealed by UHPLC/MS and NMR (doi: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11306-013-0585-y)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":80,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":88,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":106,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":177,"length":41}],"data":{}},{"key":"bvtau","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8467507023EF11E9A87E8534AA35DEC0","url":"https://doi.org/10.1101/502971"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E030A0F023EF11E9A2E8F32E236663DD","url":"https://doi.org/10.1007/s11306-013-0585-y"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2mqtn","text":"For each sample, label an extraction vial and a corresponding LC-MS glass vial","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c34dc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3p56a","text":"When you are collecting samples, process an additional extraction vial and solvent as normally would to use as a blank for LC-MS","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ev6t7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8fev4","text":"Consider pre-weighing (before extraction) and re-weighing (after extraction) each labeled extraction vial  to make measuring the leaf weight easier","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare the Acylsugar Extraction Solution (3:3:2 Acetonitrile:Isopropanol:water, 0.1% formic acid, 1 µM telmisartan (internal standard)) To prepare 1000 mL Acylsugar Extraction Solution, combine: 375 mL Acetonitrile 375 mL Isopropanol 250 mL ddH 2 O 1136.38 µL Formic Acid (88%) (0.1% final conc.) 1000 µL 1 mM Telmisartan (dissolved in 100% DMSO; final conc. 1 μM) We suggest always making fresh solution. However, in our experience, extraction buffer could be stored short term at 4℃ or -20℃ with no obvious issues. We did not carry out degradation curve and do not claim to know the best storage condition.
Dispense 1 ml of Acylsugar Extraction Solution into the labeled extraction vial

Using a fine scissors or forceps, cut 1-2 small new leaflets and immediately place them in the designated extraction vial Clean the forceps and/or scissors with Acetonitrile and a Kimwipe between samples.
Tighten the cap well and gently agitate the tube on a rocker for 00:02:00

Remove the vial from the rocker and immediately pipette the extraction solution into the corresponding LC-MS glass vial and seal it with the cap. Make sure the leaflet remains in the extraction vial. Acylsugars are not green, however, disturbance of chlorophyll and/or other metabolites might make the extraction solution turn green-ish looking. Based on our experience, this does not affect the extraction and qualification of acylsugars.

Store the LC-MS vials at -20 °C. For quantification purpose: Place uncapped extraction vials (containing leaf tissue) in a drying cabinet (~40°C) and discard the extraction vial cap. Weigh dried leaf tissue after 3 days or after thoroughly dried

To analyze extraction on LC-MS, we use the following LC-MS system and methods: Waters Acquity UPLC Waters Xevo G2-XS QToF mass spectrometer Column: Ascentis Express C18 HPLC column (10 cm x 2.1 mm, 2.7 µm) Oven temp: 40 °C Flow rate: 0.3 mL/min Solvent A: 10 mM ammonium formate, pH 2.8 Solvent B: 100% acetonitrile To prepare 1000 mL of 10 mM Ammonium Formate, pH 2.8: Add 630.6 mg of Ammonium Formate to 950 mL of water Adjust the pH of the solution to 2.8 using formic acid Add water up to 1000 mL Table 1. 7-min LC method %s %s Table 2. 21-min LC method %s %s If you are not familiar with LC-MS, we highly recommend getting on-site help from an experienced person.

To analyze extraction on LC-MS, we use the following MS settings: For negative ion-mode electrospray ionization: Capillary voltage: 2.00 kV Source temperature: 100°C Desolvation temperature: 350°C Desolvation nitrogen gas flow rate: 600 liters/h Cone voltage: 35V Mass range: m/z 50 to 1000 with spectra accumulated at 0.1 seconds/function Collision energies: Function 1: 0V Function 2: 15V Function 3: 35V For positive ion-mode electrospray ionization: Capillary voltage: 3.00 kV Source temperature: 100°C Desolvation temperature: 350°C Desolvation nitrogen gas flow rate: 600 liters/h Cone voltage: 35V Mass range: m/z 50 to 1000 with spectra accumulated at 0.1 seconds/function Collision energies: Function 1: 0V Function 2: 15V Function 3: 45V Fore more detail, please refer to the experiment files: ESI- experiment file.txt ESI+ experiment file.txt If you are not familiar with LC-MS, we highly recommend getting on-site help from an experienced person.

For a list of manuscripts with useful information regarding acylsugar identification (masses, fragmentation patterns and quanitifcation methods), please refer to the description page of this protocol.
creator: Yann-Ru Lou
